title: `nixery.dev` style images with non-sequential layers break `buildah bud`
body:
**Description**

```shell
$ cat Dockerfile
FROM nixery.dev/shell
RUN ls
$ buildah bud .
STEP 1/2: FROM nixery.dev/shell
STEP 2/2: RUN ls
bin
dev
etc
lib
libexec
nix
nix-support
proc
run
share
sys
tmp
usr
COMMIT
error committing container for step {Env:[SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[ls] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN ls Original:RUN ls}: error copying layers and metadata for container "b2dfabd70db97640d06962855cf9bef4cf618c883cf005b495c2e86904792f67": initializing source containers-storage:shell-working-container: internal error: history lists 1 non-empty layers, but we have 10 layers on disk
```

I believe `nixery.dev` packages together many layers in one manifest that do not depend on each other; which _seems_ to be what's confusing buildah here? 

```shell
$ apt list buildah
Listing... Done
buildah/experimental,now 1.26.1+ds1-1 amd64 [installed]
buildah/testing,unstable 1.23.1+ds1-2+b1 i386
$ buildah version
Version:         1.26.1
Go Version:      go1.18.2
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.21.1
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 10:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
$ cat /etc/*release
PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux bookworm/sid"
NAME="Debian GNU/Linux"
ID=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.debian.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://www.debian.org/support"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.debian.org/"
$ uname -a
Linux dynast 5.17.0-3-amd64 #1 SMP PREEMPT Debian 5.17.11-1 (2022-05-26) x86_64 GNU/Linux
$ cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/user/1000"
graphroot = "/home/sohum/.local/share/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```

comments.body:
Absolutely!

Additionally, there appear to be environment issues under these images too — `buildah` seems to build them with a missing `HOME`. I'm not sure if this is this bug or a different bug; let me know if I should open a new issue for this. 

```shell
$ cat Dockerfile
FROM nixery.dev/shell
RUN env

$ docker build . 
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.048kB
Step 1/2 : FROM nixery.dev/shell
 ---> e0dc353c8e2d
Step 2/2 : RUN env
 ---> Running in 3473c9fd7497
HOSTNAME=3473c9fd7497
PWD=/
HOME=/
SHLVL=0
SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
_=/usr/bin/env
Removing intermediate container 3473c9fd7497
 ---> b5ca3705948c
Successfully built b5ca3705948c

$ buildah bud .
STEP 1/2: FROM nixery.dev/shell
STEP 2/2: RUN env
HOSTNAME=6871a14d19d6
PWD=/
SHLVL=0
SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
_=/usr/bin/env
COMMIT
error committing container for step {Env:[SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[env] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN env Original:RUN env}: error copying layers and metadata for container "6871a14d19d63249910dc5f0d4e5727009ef794535f81cd131ef44cae8f61e9a": initializing source containers-storage:shell-working-container: internal error: history lists 1 non-empty layers, but we have 10 layers on disk

$ buildah bud --env HOME=/ .
STEP 1/2: FROM nixery.dev/shell
STEP 2/2: RUN env
HOSTNAME=4be8aabea01c
PWD=/
SHLVL=0
SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
_=/usr/bin/env
COMMIT
error committing container for step {Env:[SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[env] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN env Original:RUN env}: error copying layers and metadata for container "4be8aabea01c25b3be8d9c2e5af2a9e351fe4585d54d2bb97242928ea071efed": initializing source containers-storage:shell-working-container: internal error: history lists 1 non-empty layers, but we have 10 layers on disk
```

Hi @SohumB, Would you be able to tell us from which build tool  `nixery.dev/shell` was created cause it contains 10 layers but `History` in spec marks only one. This usually does not happens I am just wondering which build tool was used. But anyways since `history` is optional in `image-spec` so I think buildah should drop this sanity check https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/config.md#properties so following diff should fix this for you.

```diff
diff --git a/image.go b/image.go
index e3668bd0..3c24e709 100644
--- a/image.go
+++ b/image.go
@@ -558,17 +558,6 @@ func (i *containerImageRef) NewImageSource(ctx context.Context, sc *types.System
        dimage.History = append(dimage.History, dnews)
        appendHistory(i.postEmptyLayers)
 
-       // Sanity check that we didn't just create a mismatch between non-empty layers in the
-       // history and the number of diffIDs.
-       expectedDiffIDs := expectedOCIDiffIDs(oimage)
-       if len(oimage.RootFS.DiffIDs) != expectedDiffIDs {
-               return nil, errors.Errorf("internal error: history lists %d non-empty layers, but we have %d layers on disk", expectedDiffIDs, len(oimage.RootFS.DiffIDs))
-       }
-       expectedDiffIDs = expectedDockerDiffIDs(dimage)
-       if len(dimage.RootFS.DiffIDs) != expectedDiffIDs {
-               return nil, errors.Errorf("internal error: history lists %d non-empty layers, but we have %d layers on disk", expectedDiffIDs, len(dimage.RootFS.DiffIDs))
-       }
-
        // Encode the image configuration blob.
        oconfig, err := json.Marshal(&oimage)
        if err != nil {
```

But changes has associated issue in the past so just getting ack from @nalind on this.

The `History` field _is_ optional in OCI, but this is the first time I've seen an image without one.  If we don't have one, we're probably better off not creating one with just the entry for our new layer, to avoid creating confusion in tools that consume the result.
The particular check you're proposing we drop is the end-result of a _long_ process getting the surrounding logic right, particularly when dealing with v2s1 base images, so I'm reluctant to just throw it away.

I don't precisely know the details of how `nixery.dev` works, but the short version is that it's an experiment in building images out of independent layers to maximise cache efficiency. When you request, say, `nixery.dev/git/htop`, the server uses the `nix` package manager to generate `git` and `htop`'s independent filesystems, and then just puts them together in a single OCI image.

There's some documentation on this process [here](https://nixery.dev/nixery.html) and [here](https://grahamc.com/blog/nix-and-layered-docker-images) and [here](https://storage.googleapis.com/nixdoc/nixery-layers.html). The source code is [here](https://github.com/tazjin/nixery).
PR https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4028 should close use-cases like this with `buildah build --add-history=false .`
Note that Docker happily builds the upper Dockerfile.  If users have to do an extra step, Buildah would break compat.

> Note that Docker happily builds the upper Dockerfile. If users have to do an extra step, Buildah would break compat.

@vrothberg Ah i see although it does not looks like docker is doing correct thing cause it appends new history even when base has a `history:null` but in-order to maintain parity :) added another commit for docker-parity which still adds history even if baseImage history is `null` and ignore sanity check so `buildah build .` should also work for this issue once this is merged https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4028 but still recommend way would be `--add-history=false`.
